Bus Timings

F2 Bus Timings Etisalat MS to Al Muhaisnah

September 14, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Bus F02 provides a convenient transport link between Etisalat Bus Station and Al Muhaisnah 4, ensuring smooth and accessible travel across key areas in Dubai. Whether you’re a resident commuting daily or a visitor exploring the city, Bus F02 offers an efficient way to connect with important destinations. With frequent services and strategically placed stops, Bus F02 plays a crucial role in Dubai’s public transport system.

Operational Details

Bus F02 operates consistently throughout the day to accommodate various travel needs, ensuring flexibility for both early risers and those commuting later in the evening. Below is the timetable for Bus F02:

From Etisalat MS From Al Muhaisnah
04:19 04:34
05:05 05:20
05:30 05:45
06:00 06:15
06:22 06:37
06:44 06:59
07:04 07:19
07:21 07:36
07:38 07:53
07:55 08:10
08:12 08:27
08:29 08:44
08:49 09:04
09:09 09:24
09:29 09:44
09:49 10:04
10:09 10:24
10:29 10:44
10:49 11:04
11:09 11:24
11:29 11:44
11:49 12:04
12:09 12:24
12:29 12:44
12:54 13:09
13:19 13:34
13:44 13:59
14:10 14:25
14:35 14:50
15:05 15:20
15:30 15:45
16:00 16:15
16:20 16:35
16:50 17:05

17:30 17:45

18:30 18:45
18:50 19:05

21:30 21:45
22:00 22:15
22:30 22:45
23:00 23:15
23:30 23:45
00:16 00:31

Bus F2 Timetable

To view the full schedule, you can visit the official RTA website, or simply follow this link for F2 Bus Timings.

Ticket Pricing: Passengers can pay for their journey on Bus F02 using Dubai’s Nol card, the city’s unified contactless payment system for public transportation. The fares are zone-based, starting at AED 3 for a single zone trip. Nol cards can be topped up at RTA service centers or vending machines across the city, providing a convenient and cashless way to travel.

Route Overview

The journey on Bus F02 starts at Etisalat Bus Station, a key hub in Dubai’s transport network, and ends at Al Muhaisnah 4. Below are some of the notable stops along the route:

  1. Etisalat Bus Station The starting point, offering easy access to other bus routes and the Dubai Metro.

  2. Emirates Cooperative Society A convenient stop for shopping needs.

  3. 22nd Street A busy area frequently used by commuters.

  4. Masjid Bin Tahnoon Located near a local mosque, serving the surrounding community.

  5. Qutami Al Suweidi Masjid Another mosque stop along the route for worshippers.

  6. Technical School An important stop for students and staff heading to the technical institute.

  7. FEWA A key stop for those visiting or working at the Federal Electricity and Water Authority.

  8. Jamal Building Serving local residents and visitors to the area.

  9. Lootah Building A residential stop for commuters living nearby.

  10. Oxford School Gate 4 A stop catering to students and staff of Oxford School.

  11. Awqaf Housings A residential community stop along the route.

  12. Emirates Housing Another housing stop for local residents.

  13. New Housing Serving a new residential development in the area.

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Lebanese Private School A stop near a well-known educational institution.

  2. 76th Street A major street with two stops along the route.

  3. Public Transport Agency Connecting passengers to additional public transport services.

  4. Dubai Transport Main Office A significant stop for those working in or visiting the transport department.

  5. Etisalat Bus Station The final stop, completing the loop.

Accessibility Features

Bus F02 is equipped with modern accessibility features to ensure comfortable travel for differently-abled passengers. With low-floor buses for easy boarding, designated seating, and audio-visual systems to assist those with hearing or visual impairments, the route is designed to accommodate all travelers.

Connectivity and Intersections

Bus F02 connects seamlessly with other bus routes and metro services, particularly at Etisalat Bus Station. This connectivity ensures passengers can transfer between different transport modes easily, making it a vital part of Dubai’s wider public transport system.

Nearby Attractions and Landmarks

Key points along the F02 route include:

  • Emirates Cooperative Society

  • Technical School
  • Dubai Transport Main Office

Journey Experience

A journey on the F2 bus route from Etisalat Metro Station to Al Muhaisnah offers a long yet engaging trip through some of Dubai’s diverse neighborhoods. Starting at the busy Etisalat Metro Station, the bus travels through residential and commercial areas, passing by local shops and community hubs.

As you make your way toward Al Muhaisnah, known for its vibrant mix of cultural influences, you’ll see a blend of modern developments alongside traditional areas, reflecting Dubai’s growth and diversity.

Tips and Travel Adviced

To make the most of your journey on Bus F02, consider traveling during off-peak hours to avoid congestion. Keep your Nol card topped up to ensure a smooth boarding process, and don’t forget to check the RTA app for real-time updates on bus timings and routes. To further enhance your travel experience, plan your trip in advance by familiarizing yourself with key stops and landmarks along the route.

Bus F02 offers a reliable and efficient travel option, connecting Etisalat Bus Station and Al Muhaisnah 4 while providing access to essential stops along the way. Whether you’re a daily commuter or an occasional traveler, this route ensures a convenient and eco-friendly way to explore Dubai.

For more information on schedules and updates, visit the RTA website or download the RTA app.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: September 14, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej
